case_title: HYATT CORPORATION A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. UNITE HERE! LOCAL2 A LABOR ORGANIZATION et al

docket_entries.description: ORDER CONTINUING THE TRIAL DATE AND ALL RELATED DATES PURSUANT TO STIPULATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT UNITE HERE! LOCAL2 A LABOR ORGANIZATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 12.1 SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 4 AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS 15 IN ITS ENTIRETY AS MODIFIED: GRANTED. IN RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 12.1 AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 4, PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE INCLUDES "VARIOUS UNIDENTIFIED GUESTS OR PATRONS OF PLAINTIFF." THE DISCLOSURE OF NAMES AND ADDRESSESS OF POTENTIAL WITNESSES IS A ROUTINE AND ESSENTIAL PART OF PRETRIAL DISCOVERY. PUERTO V. SUPERIOR COURT, 158 CAL. APP. 4TH 1242, 1250 (2008). IN FACT, PLAINTIFF HAS ALREADY AGREED TO SUCH DISCLOSURES WITH REGARD TO CERTAIN HOTEL PATRONS. THERE IS NO BASIS TO DISTINGUISH THE DISCLOSURE OF THESE WITNESSES FROM OTHER SIMILARLY SITUATED POTENTIAL HOTEL GUEST WITNESSES, OR TO LIMIT PLAINTIFF'S DISCLOSURE TO THOSE HOTEL PATRON WITNESSES IT HAS ALREADY AGREED TO IDENTIFY. BALANCING THE PRIVACY INTERESTS OF THE GUESTS WITH THE INTERESTS OF THE REQUESTING PARTY, FAIRNESS TO THE LITIGANTS, AND THE CONSEQUENCES OF GRANTING ACCESS TO THE INFORMATION, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION, SUBJECT TO CERTAIN CONDITIONS TO PROTECT THE PRIVACY OF THE INFORMATION SOUGHT. WITHIN 20 DAYS, PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES BY PROVIDING THE NAMES AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF ALL GUESTS WHO WERE PRESENT DURING THE RELEVANT DATES. THESE RESPONSES ARE SUBJECT TO THE FOLLOWING LIMITATIONS: (1) THIS INFORMATION SHALL BE SUBJECT TO A PROTECTIVE ORDER USED ONLY BY THE ATTORNEYS AND THEIR AGENTS FOR USE IN THIS CASE ONLY, AND THE INFORMATION SHALL NOT BE SHARED WITH OR DISSEMINATED TO THE DEFENDANT UNION, ITS MEMBERS OR EMPLOYEES, EXCEPT AS MAY BE DIVULGED IN A JUDICIAL PROCEEDING. PARTIES HAVE STIPULATED TO THE ADDITIONAL PROTECTIVE MEASURE OF AN OPT-OUT PRIVACY NOTICE TO BE SENT TO HOTEL PATRONS. (DEFENDANT'S MPA AT 8, L. 7-11.) IN ADDITION, INFORMATION TO BE P

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 12.1 Special Interrogatory 4 And Request For Production Of Documents 15
Off Calendar Jan-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew David Ross, Esq. for defendant and moving party appearing by telephone (no card);
Kamran Mirrafati, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw LLP, Tel. 310.201.5208, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT UNITE HERE! LOCAL2 A LABOR ORGANIZATION'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 12.1 Special Interrogatory 4 And Request For Production Of Documents 15 in its entirety as modified: Granted. In response to Form Interrogatory 12.1 and Special Interrogatory 4, Plaintiff's response includes "various unidentified guests or patrons of Plaintiff." The disclosure of names and addressess of potential witnesses is a routine and essential part of pretrial discovery. Puerto v. Superior Court, 158 Cal. App. 4th 1242, 1250 (2008). In fact, Plaintiff has already agreed to such disclosures with regard to certain hotel patrons. There is no basis to distinguish the disclosure of these witnesses from other similarly situated potential hotel guest witnesses, or to limit Plaintiff's disclosure to those hotel patron witnesses it has already agreed to identify. Balancing the privacy interests of the guests with the interests of the requesting party, fairness to the litigants, and the consequences of granting access to the information, the Court grants the motion, subject to certain conditions to protect the privacy of the information sought. Within 20 days, Plaintiff must provide supplemental responses by providing the names and contact information of all guests who were present during the relevant dates. These responses are subject to the following limitations: (1) this information shall be subject to a protective order used only by the attorneys and their agents for use in this case only, and the information shall not be shared with or disseminated to the defendant union, its members or employees, except as may be divulged in a judicial proceeding. Parties have stipulated to the additional protective measure of an opt-out privacy notice to be sent to hotel patrons. (Defendant's MPA at 8, l. 7-11.) In addition, information to be provided shall relate to individuals who were hotel patrons during the 3-day and 5-day strikes and the dates where there was protracted picketing for 12 hours or more. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and email it to the court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
